title: rubus × agrestis
intro: Rubus × agrestis, also known as wild bramble or wild raspberry, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the rose family and is a cross between the European raspberry (Rubus idaeus) and the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis). This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to aid in wound healing and to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Rubus × agrestis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant properties, which can contribute to overall well-being. Rubus × agrestis can be prepared into herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
